title: You can just read 25 books
body: Andreessen Horowitz compiled book recommendations from founders and partners
Reading approximately 25 books can help outsiders compete with established experts

title: Overview: A Curated Reading List for Competitive Edge
body: 14 founders and partners contributed recommendations
Books span business strategy, historical narratives, decision-making frameworks, and philosophy
Ali Ghodsi provided six recommendations including management and innovation titles

title: Contributors: Industry Leaders
body: Recommendations compiled from 14 founders and partners
Leaders from Flock Safety, Applied Intuition, Decagon included
Leaders from Databricks, Kalshi, and Abridge also contributed

title: Business Strategy Titles
body: Sam Walton: Made in America offers retail and entrepreneurship insights
Good to Great examines company transformation principles
Who focuses on talent selection and hiring strategies

title: Historical Narratives for Leadership Lessons
body: The Wright Brothers chronicles innovation and persistence
Napoleon: A Life explores strategic leadership
Endurance details Shackleton's Antarctic survival and crisis management

title: Decision-Making Frameworks
body: Decisive provides structured approaches to making better choices
Monetizing Innovation offers frameworks for pricing and value capture
These titles help develop systematic thinking for business challenges

title: Philosophy and Reflection
body: Man's Search for Meaning by Viktor Frankl explores purpose and resilience
In Praise of Shadows examines aesthetics and cultural perspectives
The Magic Mountain offers philosophical depth through literary narrative

title: Ali Ghodsi's Six Recommendations
body: Ali Ghodsi recommended six books total
The Effective Executive by Peter Drucker on management effectiveness
Competing Against Luck by Clayton Christensen on innovation theory

title: The Effective Executive
body: Recommended by Ali Ghodsi from Databricks
Peter Drucker's classic on management effectiveness
Focuses on productive executive habits and time management

title: Competing Against Luck
body: Clayton Christensen's framework for understanding innovation
Recommended by Ali Ghodsi
Explores jobs-to-be-done theory for product development

title: Sam Walton: Made in America
body: Autobiography of Walmart founder Sam Walton
Featured in business strategy category
Covers retail innovation and customer-centric business building

title: Good to Great
body: Jim Collins' research on company transformation
Featured among business strategy recommendations
Analyzes what separates good companies from great ones

title: The Wright Brothers
body: David McCullough's account of aviation pioneers
Featured in historical narratives section
Chronicles innovation, experimentation, and breakthrough achievement

title: Man's Search for Meaning
body: Viktor Frankl's philosophical work from Holocaust experience
Featured in philosophy category
Explores finding purpose and meaning in extreme adversity

title: The Core Insight: 25 Books
body: Reading approximately 25 books can help outsiders compete with established experts
Curated list from Andreessen Horowitz network of 14 leaders
Strategic reading as competitive advantage in business and leadership
